western.definition: Minimal time from problem to action; rapid iteration cycles; quick response prioritized.

relational.definition: Appropriate timing based on system readiness; decision speed matched to consequence timescale; pattern recognition enabling efficient response.
assumptions: Right timing matters more than maximum speed.

hidden_variables.name: Preparation quality
hidden_variables.description: How well the actor is conditioned for the moment of decision.

hidden_variables.name: Consequence scope
hidden_variables.description: How far the decision's effects spread before they can be reversed.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Iteration speed assumes consequences stay local.

hidden_variables.name: Learning integration
hidden_variables.description: How thoroughly past iterations have been metabolized into pattern recognition.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: 'Move fast' optimizes throughput at the expense of consolidation.

hidden_variables.name: Relationship impacts
hidden_variables.description: How decisions disturb the trust and reciprocity networks the actor depends on.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: These costs surface only in the next few cycles.

translation_protocol.when_to_translate: When 'speed' is being valued without reference to the consequence timescale or the cost of premature action.
translation_protocol.how: Ask: 'How long do the consequences of this decision propagate, and is the decision being matched to that timescale or to a faster one?'
